meta.source: Supreme Court Rules, 2013 (GSR 368(E), dated 27th May 2014, as amended)
meta.description: Comprehensive knowledge base for all Supreme Court of India filing types

supreme_court.name: Supreme Court of India
supreme_court.filing_counter_hours: 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM (no work after 4:30 PM)

slp_civil.name: Special Leave Petition (Civil)
slp_civil.full_name: Special Leave Petition under Article 136 of the Constitution of India

limitation.from_refusal_of_certificate: 60 days from date of order of refusal
limitation.from_judgment_order: 90 days from date of judgment or order

court_fee.note: Higher fee (Rs.5000) for tax/company/arbitration/banking/IP matters (Part IV of Third Schedule)

document_order: Special Leave Petition in Form No. 28 (with affidavit)

validation_checklist.description: Court fee stamp of Rs.1500 (or Rs.5000 for special subject categories) must be affixed
validation_checklist.rule: Order VIII Rule 8, Third Schedule Part II

validation_checklist.description: Petition must be in prescribed Form No. 28
Papers in prescribed order: List of Dates → Certified Copy → SLP Form 28 → Appendix → Annexures
Certified copy of impugned judgment must be attached
Affidavit in support of statement of facts must be attached
validation_checklist.rule: Order XXI Rule 4(ii) and Order IX

validation_checklist.description: Vakalatnama duly executed and certified by AOR
Chronological list of dates must appear first in paper book
Statement whether any prior SLP against same order was filed, with affidavit
Statement on availability and status of Letters Patent/Writ Appeal
A4/demy-foolscap paper, double-line spacing, one side only
Appendix with English text of relevant constitutional/statutory provisions
Annexures individually indexed with page numbers; certified or affirmed copies
1 spare set at initial filing; 3+ sets with declaration on re-filing
If beyond 60/90 days, application for condonation of delay required
validation_checklist.rule: Order XXI Rule 1 and Order V Rule 1(11)

validation_checklist.description: All non-English documents must have certified English translation
All pages must carry sequential page numbers

slp_criminal.name: Special Leave Petition (Criminal)

limitation.from_refusal_of_certificate: 60 days from date of order of refusal
limitation.from_judgment_order: 90 days from date of judgment or order
limitation.note: Different provisions for death sentence matters

court_fee.note: Criminal proceedings: NO court fee required

key_notes: No court fee payable in criminal proceedings
If accused in jail: surrender certificate from jail authorities required
Vakalatnama must be filed from jail if accused is in custody
For POCSO/rape cases: victim name MUST be redacted throughout

validation_checklist.description: Criminal proceedings: No court fee required
Certified copy of impugned judgment/conviction order must be attached
Affidavit in support verifying facts must be filed
validation_checklist.rule: Order XXII Rule 2 and Order IX

validation_checklist.description: Vakalatnama by AOR; if accused in jail, filed through jail authorities
Chronological list of dates including arrest, trial, conviction dates
If accused in custody: surrender certificate from Superintendent of Jail required
Statement whether any prior petition against same order was filed
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only
In POCSO/rape/sexual offence cases: victim name and identity MUST be redacted throughout ALL documents
Non-English documents (FIR, charge sheet, judgment) must have certified translation
If beyond 90 days from order, condonation of delay application required

writ_article32.name: Writ Petition (Article 32)
writ_article32.full_name: Writ Petition for enforcement of Fundamental Rights under Article 32

limitation.note: No statutory limitation period, but laches doctrine applies
limitation.general: File at earliest opportunity; delay must be explained

court_fee.note: No fee for Habeas Corpus or criminal proceedings

validation_checklist.description: Court fee of Rs.500 for Writ Petition (except Habeas Corpus and criminal)
Specific fundamental right(s) under Part III of Constitution must be invoked
Specific writ sought (mandamus/certiorari/prohibition/quo warranto/habeas corpus) must be stated
Affidavit verifying facts of the petition
validation_checklist.rule: Order IX and Order XXXV Rule 1

validation_checklist.description: Vakalatnama by AOR
Petitioner must show standing — personal violation of fundamental right or PIL grounds
Writ must lie against State/instrumentality of State under Article 12
Statement on whether alternative remedies (High Court writ) were availed
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only
Chronological list of material dates

writ_habeas_corpus.name: Habeas Corpus Petition
writ_habeas_corpus.full_name: Writ of Habeas Corpus under Article 32

limitation.note: No limitation period — urgent matter of personal liberty

court_fee.note: NO court fee for Habeas Corpus

validation_checklist.description: Habeas Corpus: No court fee required
Name, date of arrest/detention, place of detention, authority must be stated
Specific law/order under which detained must be stated
Affidavit by detainee or close relative verifying facts
Vakalatnama by AOR; if detainee cannot sign, relative can give authority
Specific grounds challenging legality of detention must be stated

appeal_civil.name: Civil Appeal
appeal_civil.full_name: Civil Appeal before the Supreme Court

limitation.general: As per Limitation Act 1963 — 90 days from date of judgment (certificate cases)

validation_checklist.description: Court fee stamp of Rs.1500 must be affixed
Certificate of leave from High Court (Article 133/134A) or SLP converted to appeal
validation_checklist.rule: Article 133/134A, Constitution and Order XIX Rule 1

validation_checklist.description: Certified copy of impugned judgment and decree must be filed
Affidavit in support of appeal
Vakalatnama by AOR
Chronological list of dates
Specific grounds of appeal, questions of law must be clearly stated
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only
Appeal must be within limitation or condonation application required
validation_checklist.rule: Order XIX Rule 2 and Limitation Act

appeal_criminal.name: Criminal Appeal
appeal_criminal.full_name: Criminal Appeal before the Supreme Court

limitation.death_sentence: No limitation for death sentence appeals

court_fee.note: No court fee in criminal matters

validation_checklist.description: Criminal Appeal: No court fee required
Certified copy of conviction judgment and sentence order must be filed
Affidavit verifying facts of appeal
Vakalatnama by AOR; surrender certificate if accused in jail
Specific grounds challenging conviction/sentence must be stated
In rape/POCSO/sexual offence cases: victim name must be redacted throughout
Current bail or surrender status of accused must be stated
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

limitation.note: No specific limitation, but file at earliest; laches may be considered

validation_checklist.description: Court fee of Rs.500 for Transfer Petition (Civil)
Specific grounds for transfer must be stated — apprehension of prejudice, convenience of parties, common question of law
Details of case(s) pending transfer — court, case number, stage — must be provided
Affidavit verifying facts of petition
Vakalatnama by AOR
Statement whether notice has been given to other parties in pending cases
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

transfer_criminal.name: Transfer Petition (Criminal)
transfer_criminal.full_name: Transfer Petition (Criminal) under Section 406 CrPC / Section 447 BNSS

court_fee.note: No court fee in criminal matters

validation_checklist.description: Criminal Transfer Petition: No court fee required
Specific grounds: safety of witnesses, fair trial apprehension, convenience, common question must be stated
validation_checklist.rule: Section 406 CrPC / Section 447 BNSS and Order XXXIX

validation_checklist.description: Details of criminal case(s) pending transfer — court, FIR/case number, sections, stage
Affidavit verifying facts
Vakalatnama by AOR
In POCSO/rape/sexual offence cases: victim identity must be redacted throughout
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

contempt_civil.name: Contempt Petition (Civil)
contempt_civil.full_name: Contempt Petition for Civil Contempt of Supreme Court's order
contempt_civil.order_reference: Order XLVII, Supreme Court Rules 2013 and Contempt of Courts Act 1971

limitation.general: Within 1 year from date of alleged contempt

validation_checklist.description: Court fee of Rs.500 required for Contempt Petition
Certified copy of SC order allegedly violated must be annexed
Specific acts/omissions constituting contempt must be described with dates
Affidavit verifying acts of contempt
Vakalatnama by AOR
Full names and designation of alleged contemnors must be stated
Contempt petition must be within 1 year from date of alleged contemptuous act
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

contempt_criminal.name: Contempt Petition (Criminal)
contempt_criminal.full_name: Contempt Petition for Criminal Contempt of Court
contempt_criminal.order_reference: Order XLVII, Supreme Court Rules 2013 and Contempt of Courts Act 1971

limitation.general: Within 1 year from date of alleged contempt

court_fee.note: No court fee for criminal contempt

validation_checklist.description: Criminal Contempt: No court fee
Specific acts scandalising court, prejudicing judicial proceedings, or obstruction of justice must be described
Copies of publications, statements, videos, or other evidence of contempt must be annexed
Affidavit verifying facts of contempt
Vakalatnama by AOR
Consent of Attorney General of India required to initiate criminal contempt (unless court takes suo motu)
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

review_petition.name: Review Petition
review_petition.full_name: Petition for Review of Judgment/Order of the Supreme Court
review_petition.order_reference: Order XLVII, Supreme Court Rules 2013 and Article 137

limitation.general: 30 days from date of judgment/order sought to be reviewed

validation_checklist.description: Court fee of Rs.200 required for Review Petition
Grounds of review under Order XLVII Rule 1 CPC — error apparent on face of record, discovery of new matter, any other sufficient reason — must be specifically stated
validation_checklist.rule: Order XLVII Rule 1 CPC and Article 137

validation_checklist.description: Certified copy of SC judgment/order sought to be reviewed must be annexed
Affidavit verifying grounds for review
Vakalatnama by AOR
Review petition must be within 30 days from date of SC judgment; if delayed, condonation required
Review cannot raise fresh grounds not argued in original hearing — verify grounds are review-worthy
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

curative_petition.name: Curative Petition
curative_petition.full_name: Curative Petition before the Supreme Court
curative_petition.order_reference: Rupa Ashok Hurra v. Ashok Hurra (2002) 4 SCC 388

limitation.note: Only after review petition dismissed or after review jurisdiction exhausted

validation_checklist.description: Court fee required for Curative Petition
Must state that review petition was filed and dismissed; curative only lies after review dismissed
validation_checklist.rule: Rupa Ashok Hurra v. Ashok Hurra (2002) 4 SCC 388

validation_checklist.description: Grounds must show: (i) violation of principles of natural justice, or (ii) judge with bias participated — very high threshold
Petition must be certified by a Senior Advocate that grounds are made out
Both original SC judgment AND review dismissal order must be annexed
Affidavit verifying facts and grounds
Vakalatnama by AOR
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

pil.name: PIL — Public Interest Litigation
pil.full_name: Writ Petition (Public Interest Litigation) under Article 32
pil.order_reference: Order XXXV, Supreme Court Rules 2013 and PIL Guidelines

limitation.note: No statutory limitation; must not be inordinately delayed

court_fee.note: Rs.500 court fee (may be waived in genuine PIL)

validation_checklist.description: Court fee of Rs.500 (unless fee waiver sought in PIL)
Must establish genuine public interest — not private dispute or publicity stunt
Specific fundamental rights (often Articles 14, 19, 21) being violated must be identified
Petitioner acting bona fide in public interest; no personal gain or vendetta
Union of India/State and concerned Ministry/Department must be proper respondents
Affidavit verifying facts and affirming bona fide public interest motivation
Vakalatnama by AOR
PIL must have specific, enforceable prayer — not vague directions
Documentary evidence, reports, RTI responses supporting the public interest claim should be annexed
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

election_petition.name: Election Petition
election_petition.full_name: Election Petition under Representation of the People Act, 1951

limitation.general: 45 days from date of election of returned candidate (Presidential/VP elections only before SC)
limitation.rule_reference: Sections 80, 81 — Representation of the People Act 1951

court_fee.note: Deposit of security for costs required

validation_checklist.description: Security deposit of Rs.2000 required for Election Petition before SC
SC has original jurisdiction only for Presidential and VP election petitions
Election petition must be filed within 45 days of election of returned candidate
Specific corrupt practices or violations of RPA 1951 must be stated with particulars
Affidavit verifying allegations of corrupt practice in prescribed form
Vakalatnama by AOR
A4 paper, double spacing, one side only

document_format.legibility: Fairly and legibly written, type-written or lithographed

defective_documents.process: Document placed before Registrar after notice to party
defective_documents.formal_defect: Registrar may allow rectification in his presence
defective_documents.other_defect: Party must get order from Court; time allowed up to 28 days aggregate
defective_documents.appeal: Aggrieved party may appeal to Judge in Chambers within 15 days

court_fees.payment_mode: Court fee stamp sold in Delhi ONLY
court_fees.cancellation: Officer receiving document must cancel stamp by punching figure-head
court_fees.deficiency: Registrar can allow making good deficiency within max 3 months

affidavits.knowledge_restriction: Only facts deponent can prove from own knowledge (belief must be stated)

vakalatnama.restriction: Cannot authorize another AOR if one already engaged (without proper procedure)

translation.requirement: All non-English documents must be translated
acceptable: Translation agreed to by both parties
translation.translator_oath: Translator must make oath/affirmation of correct and accurate translation

victim_name_redaction_required: Rape (IPC Section 376 and all variants including 376A, 376B, 376C, 376D)
POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act 2012)
Any offence involving a minor victim (below 18 years)
sensitive_case_types.legal_basis: Section 228A IPC / Section 72 BNS 2023 · Supreme Court Guidelines 2018 · POCSO Act 2012

subject_categories_higher_fee.description: SLP Civil matters in these categories attract Rs.5000 court fee (Part IV of Third Schedule)
categories: Direct Tax Matters (Income Tax, Wealth Tax, Gift Tax)
Indirect Tax Matters (Customs, Excise, Service Tax, GST)
